fresh_facts.claim: U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iranian targets intensified on April 1-2, 2026, with President Trump announcing an impending end to Operation Epic Fury within 2-3 weeks.
CENTCOM reported over 12,300 targets hit, 155+ Iranian vessels damaged or destroyed, and 13,000 combat flights flown as of Wednesday, April 1, 2026.
U.S. forces conducted B-52 bomber strikes on Isfahan and initiated overland B-52 flights over Iran targeting factories, warehouses, nuclear labs, and defense infrastructure.
Two additional MQ-9 Reaper drones were lost near Isfahan, bringing the total to 16 since the war began.
Israel conducted a wide-scale wave of strikes on Tehran infrastructure sites on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, with Iranian state broadcaster IRIB reporting blasts in several areas.
Israel's Army Chief Eyal Zamir warned of troop shortages amid multi-front operations against Iran, Hezbollah, and others as of April 2, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Israeli Army Chief Eyal Zamir statement

fresh_facts.claim: Iran announced a new wave of missile and drone strikes on U.S. sites in Bahrain and Kuwait and Israeli cities, though no hits were confirmed, marking the lowest 24-hour volume of such attacks since the war started.
UAE intercepted 5 Iranian missiles and 35 drones on Wednesday, April 1, adding to 438 missiles and 2,012 drones intercepted since attacks began.
Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face power plant strikes.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes on February 28, 2026, and his son Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed as successor by Tehran's Assembly of Experts.
Mojtaba Khamenei, the new Supreme Leader, demanded closure of all U.S. Middle East bases and keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed as leverage.
Iran named Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline ex-IRGC deputy commander, as new chief of the Supreme National Security Council, replacing Ali Larijani who was killed last week.
U.S. President Donald Trump announced Iran agreed to release 20 cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday, framing it as progress in negotiations.
Pakistan delivered a U.S. 15-point peace plan to Iran covering Strait reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.

Trump reported productive U.S.-Iran talks via envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner engaging a top Iranian official by phone, postponing Pentagon attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days.
Iran's parliament speaker denied that U.S.-Iran talks occurred, contradicting Trump's claims of productive negotiations.
Egypt, Pakistan, and Turkey have mediated messages between the U.S. and Iran recently.
President Pezeshkian stated on Tuesday, March 31, 2026, that Iran has the necessary will to end the war under conditions like no future aggression guarantees.
Iran denied ceasefire requests amid Trump's claims of ongoing negotiations.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth visited CENTCOM over the weekend, noting U.S. troops' readiness to continue Operation Epic Fury.
Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine highlighted the intimidating presence of thousands of U.S. soldiers and Marines positioned in the Middle East as a pressure point on Iran.
Israel accelerated strikes near Tehran, hitting the Taleghan nuclear compound for a second time.
Iran's armed forces spokesperson warned adversaries are unsafe in global tourist centers.
U.S. officials are considering operations for enriched uranium extraction or Kharg Island seizure, according to Wall Street Journal and Financial Times reports.
fresh_facts.source: Wall Street Journal and Financial Times

fresh_facts.claim: CSIS analysis dated March 27, 2026, noted Iran's shift to rapid escalation tactics.
fresh_facts.source: Center for Strategic and International Studies

fresh_facts.claim: Republican Rep. Nancy Mace opposed U.S. troop involvement in Iran following a briefing.

regime_fall.current_status: The Iranian regime faces unprecedented military devastation with 12,300+ targets destroyed and sustained B-52 strikes on critical infrastructure, while the newly appointed Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei responds with hardline security appointments and defiant rhetoric despite emerging diplomatic channels suggesting potential negotiations. Mixed signals persist as Iran simultaneously denies ceasefire talks while U.S. envoys report progress, with Trump announcing a 2-3 week timeline to conclude Operation Epic Fury.
regime_fall.last_major_event: President Trump announced an impending end to Operation Epic Fury within 2-3 weeks as U.S. and Israeli strikes intensified on April 1-2, 2026, with CENTCOM reporting over 12,300 targets hit, 155+ Iranian vessels destroyed, and B-52 bomber strikes on Isfahan and overland Iranian targets targeting nuclear facilities and defense infrastructure.
